title: Showing links between decisions
question: How should the way one decision connects to another be shown?
rationale: Show links inline in each decision's detail panel, not a separate map. 'Affects' is a loop-safe, collapsible tree: direct dependents show by default, each parent expands to its subtree with a descendant count, so a deep impact stays scannable. 'Depends on' stays one hop.

history.reason: The list-plus-panel viewer (d2) removed the map tab; connections now show as 'depends on' and 'affects' rows in the detail panel, so they're shown inline only.

history.from: Inline links showed only immediate neighbors
history.reason: The cascade job — seeing what revisiting a decision threatens — needs the full downstream chain, not just one hop. Extended 'Affects' to walk transitively (downstream only), kept it inline with no separate map.

history.from: 'Affects' shown as a flat, fully-expanded indented list of the whole downstream cascade
history.reason: The full cascade (30+ rows for big decisions like d1/d2) was a wall of indented rows. Made 'Affects' a collapsible tree — only direct dependents show, each parent expandable with a +N descendant count — so deep impact stays scannable; also fixed a bug that rendered multi-path nodes twice.

options.label: Inline links, with transitive downstream impact

tradeoffs.note: jump between linked decisions without leaving the list
'Affects' walks several hops, so you see everything a decision threatens
deep chains can get long; mitigated by downstream-only and collapsing
a second map view to build
inline links keep reviewing front and center
a map for the full web

options.label: Just save the links, show them later
